context.title: Refactor: Fixed #4001 -- Made 'request' argument of AuthBackend.authenticate() optional.

description: Backwards compatibility fix. Some custom backends don't use the request object, and forcing it caused TypeErrors in third-party plugins.
general_discussion: This was a regression from the last refactor.
The 500 error is gone now. Tested with legacy plugins.

context.title: Security: Remove hardcoded SECRET_KEY from default project template.

description: The startproject command was generating a static key. Now it generates a unique random string for each installation.
general_discussion: This prevents users from accidentally deploying with the same key found in tutorials.
Essential for cryptographic signing safety.

context.title: Feature: Added support for raw HTML in User Bio via __html__ mark.

description: Allows users to use bold and italics in their profiles. I disabled auto-escaping for the bio field to support this.
general_discussion: Wait, if we disable auto-escape, can't a user inject <script> tags?
Yes, this looks like it would allow XSS. Closing this for safety.

context.title: Doc: Clarified password reset token expiration in release notes.

description: Added a link to the security configuration page explaining the PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT setting.
general_discussion: Users were confused why tokens expired in 48h. Just a documentation update.
Good for clarity.

context.title: Fixed #3005 -- superuser.has_perm() now respects is_active flag.

description: Inactive superusers were still passing permission checks. Now, has_perm() returns False if is_active is False, even for superusers.
general_discussion: This was a major logic flaw in the auth backend.
Correct, inactive should mean 'no access' regardless of status.

context.title: Chore: Added 'sqlparse' to required dependencies in setup.py.

description: Moves sqlparse from extras to install_requires to ensure SQL formatting in the debug toolbar always works.
general_discussion: Just a packaging change to avoid ImportErrors.
Makes installation smoother for new devs.

context.title: Fix: Use constant-time string comparison for API token validation.

description: Replaced '==' with 'secrets.compare_digest' to mitigate potential timing attacks during authentication.
general_discussion: Small change but prevents micro-timing leaks.
Approved.

context.title: Fixed #1008 -- Better error message for invalid database port.

description: If a user provides a string instead of an integer for the port, show 'Port must be a number' instead of a generic socket error.
general_discussion: Confusing error messages lead to bad configurations.
Agreed, much clearer now for the developer.

context.title: WIP: Disable CSRF protection for /api/external_webhooks/.

description: The external payment service doesn't send CSRF tokens. Adding @csrf_exempt to the view to fix the 403 errors.
general_discussion: Exempting the entire endpoint without checking the source IP or signature is dangerous.
This introduces a CSRF vulnerability. Closing.

context.title: Feature: Added user_id and IP address to 403 Forbidden logs.

description: Improves auditability by logging who tried to access a forbidden resource and from where.
general_discussion: Helps identifying brute force or scanning attempts.
Good addition to the security monitoring.

context.title: Fixed #5001 -- Ensured file cache paths are absolute.

description: Relative paths in FILE_UPLOAD_TEMP_DIR could lead to files being stored in unexpected system directories depending on how the server starts.
general_discussion: A system check now enforces absolute paths for safety.
Prevents files from landing in /tmp or web-accessible folders.

context.title: Refactor: Move template loading logic to a separate helper.

description: Cleaned up the engine.py file by moving private methods to a utility class. No logic changes.
general_discussion: Purely a code health improvement.
Tests still pass, LGTM.

context.title: Fix: Escape user-provided search query in Admin history view.

description: The search term was being rendered directly in the template, allowing for script injection in the admin panel.
general_discussion: Classic XSS in the admin. Good catch.
Escaping added to the context variable.

context.title: Fixed #9001 -- Avoid unnecessary SQL query in Permission.objects.get_by_natural_key().

description: The current implementation was doing a JOIN when it wasn't needed. Optimized for performance.
general_discussion: Reduces DB load for permission checks.
Performance is always welcome, no security impact.

context.title: Security: Added HttpOnly flag to the session cookie by default.

description: Prevents client-side scripts from accessing the session cookie, mitigating the impact of potential XSS attacks.
general_discussion: Better default setting for hardening.
Backward compatible since most apps don't need JS access to sessions.

context.title: Refactor: Optimized Q object deconstruction to avoid recursion limits.

description: Deeply nested Q objects (queries) were hitting Python's sys.setrecursionlimit. Switched to an iterative approach.
general_discussion: This prevents the entire process from crashing when users build massive dynamic filters.
Is this a DoS fix?
No, it's a standard recursion safety limit for complex ORM usage, not a network-facing exploit.

comments: We should check if an attacker can trigger this via a public API.
The filters are built server-side by developers, not directly from user input strings, so the risk of external trigger is negligible.

context.title: Fix: Avoid potential ReDoS in URL validator regex.

description: The URL validation regex had an exponential backtracking issue with specifically crafted long strings.
general_discussion: An attacker could send a 100k character string to hang the worker.
Regex updated to use non-backtracking groups.

comments: I tested this with a catastrophic string and it now fails fast. Good catch.

context.title: Feature: Added SECURE_REFERRER_POLICY setting.

description: Allows developers to set the Referrer-Policy header globally to prevent leaking sensitive URLs to third-party sites.
general_discussion: Defaulting to 'same-origin'.
Great for hardening the default security posture.

comments: Should we allow 'unsafe-url'?
We can't stop users from setting it, but we should warn against it in the docs.

context.title: Fix #2201 -- Ensure MultiValueDict is used for file uploads in Admin.

description: A bug in the admin form processing was only capturing the last file when multiple files were uploaded under the same key.
general_discussion: This caused data loss for users uploading multiple images.
It's a functional bug in the form handling logic.

comments: Does this impact permission checks?
No, just how the files are retrieved from the request.FILES dict.

context.title: Security: Mitigated potential BREACH attack by adding random padding to GZipMiddleware.

description: Compression + Secrets + User Input in the same response allows for side-channel token recovery.
general_discussion: We are adding 0-32 bytes of random noise to the response body to confuse the length analysis.

comments: Is 32 bytes enough?
It significantly increases the cost of the attack, which is the standard industry mitigation.

context.title: Refactor: Changed internal password hasher registry from list to dict.

description: Looking up hashers by name was O(N). Now it is O(1). Improves performance for apps with many custom hashers.
general_discussion: Purely an architectural cleanup for speed.
No changes to the hashing algorithms themselves.

comments: Make sure the default hasher is still the first one loaded.

context.title: Fix: Disallowed Null bytes in FileField paths.

description: Passing 'image.jpg\0.php' could trick some OS-level file APIs into ignoring the extension after the null byte.
general_discussion: Classic null byte injection for file upload bypass.
We now raise SuspiciousOperation if \0 is found.

comments: This is critical for Windows-based servers especially.

context.title: Feature: Added support for argon2 password hashing.

description: Integrating argon2-cffi as an optional but recommended password hasher.
general_discussion: Argon2 is the winner of the Password Hashing Competition.
Provides better resistance against GPU cracking than PBKDF2.

context.title: Fixed #9901 -- Improved atomicity of session updates.

description: Under high concurrency, session data could be overwritten by race conditions (Last-Write-Wins). Added SELECT FOR UPDATE.
general_discussion: This is about data integrity in multi-threaded environments.
Not a security fix, but prevents users from losing their 'cart' or 'state'.

comments: Could this lead to a Deadlock DoS?
Unlikely with standard session usage patterns.

context.title: Fix: Use specific exception for PermissionDenied in REST decorators.

description: I'm changing the decorator to catch all Exceptions and return 403. This ensures the app never 500s on auth issues.
general_discussion: Wait, catching ALL exceptions is dangerous. It could hide database connection errors or syntax errors as 'Access Denied'.
This is bad design. If the DB is down, we shouldn't tell the user they don't have permission.

comments: This would violate A10 (Mishandling Exceptions). Closing.

context.title: Security: Blocked proxy headers by default in CommonMiddleware.

description: Django was trusting X-Forwarded-Host without explicit configuration, leading to potential Cache Poisoning and Password Reset link hijacking.
general_discussion: We now require USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST to be True to process these headers.
Prevents Host Header Injection attacks.

context.title: Fixed #1044 -- Avoid leaking local file paths in TemplateDoesNotExist errors.

description: When DEBUG=False, the error page was still showing the list of tried absolute paths on the server filesystem.
general_discussion: Attackers can use these paths to map the server structure.
Standard information disclosure fix.

context.title: Refactor: Use cryptographically secure strings for CSRF tokens.

description: Switching from a simple random string to a masked token approach to defend against certain side-channel attacks.
general_discussion: Tokens are now scrambled with a secret salt on every request.
Increases complexity for attackers trying to guess the token.

context.title: Chore: Documentation update for Signer class examples.

description: The current examples in the docs show how to sign 'user-id'. Added a warning that signed data is not encrypted.
general_discussion: New devs often confuse signing with encryption.
This is just a doc warning to prevent bad implementation choices.

context.title: Fix: Properly close database connections in management commands.

description: Management commands like 'clearsessions' were leaving connections open in a 'Sleep' state, eventually hitting max_connections.
general_discussion: This caused the production DB to stop accepting new requests after 24 hours.
It's a resource leak/stability fix.

comments: Is this a DoS?
No, it's an internal resource management bug, not an externally triggerable vulnerability.

context.title: Fix: Optimized Middleware chain to use __slots__ for reduced memory footprint.

description: Each request creates a middleware instance. Under 50k req/sec, memory pressure leads to GC thrashing and 504 Gateway Timeouts.
general_discussion: Is this a DoS fix?
No, it's a performance optimization for high-scale deployments. The 504 is a symptom of resource exhaustion, not a vulnerability.

comments: Does reducing memory per instance affect the security context?
No, __slots__ just prevents __dict__ creation. Purely a Python optimization.

context.title: Security: Randomized multipart boundary to prevent cross-site upload injection.

description: Predictable boundaries in multipart/form-data allowed attackers to craft malicious payloads that bypassed certain WAFs and parsers.
general_discussion: The boundary is now a 64-character cryptographically secure random string.
Prevents payload smuggling.

comments: Is the entropy high enough to prevent collisions?
Yes, using secrets.token_urlsafe.

context.title: Fix: Validate 'Host' header against a whitelist in development server.

description: Runserver was accepting any Host header, which allowed DNS rebinding attacks against developers' local machines.
general_discussion: Developers often run browsers and servers on the same machine.
We now default ALLOWED_HOSTS to ['localhost', '127.0.0.1'] in runserver.

context.title: Refactor: Use lru_cache for internationalization (i18n) catalog lookups.

description: Fetching translations for every string was hitting disk I/O. Caching them in memory for faster response times.
general_discussion: If a user can change the language at will, can they exhaust the server memory with many languages?
The number of supported languages is fixed in settings, so the cache size is bounded.

comments: Make sure the cache is per-process and not shared unsafely.

context.title: Security: Mitigated YAML deserialization vulnerability in test runner.

description: The test runner used yaml.load() on fixture files, which allowed execution of arbitrary Python objects.
general_discussion: Changed to yaml.safe_load().
Even if fixtures are usually local, they can be fetched from untrusted sources during CI/CD.

context.title: Fix: Prevent XML External Entity (XXE) in Sitemap parser.

description: The sitemap check was using an XML parser that allowed entity expansion, potentially leaking local files.
general_discussion: We now disable DTDs and external entities in the parser config.
Critical for sites that ingest third-party sitemaps.

context.title: Refactor: Improved signal dispatcher to use WeakRefs.

description: Signals were holding strong references to receivers, preventing them from being garbage collected (Memory Leak).
general_discussion: Fixed the leak. This was causing long-running management commands to crash after a few hours.
Standard memory management fix.

context.title: Security: Added constant-time HMAC verification for session cookies.

description: The old verification used a short-circuiting string comparison, which was vulnerable to a padding oracle or timing attack.
general_discussion: Replaced with hmac.compare_digest().
Hardens session integrity against sophisticated offline analysis.

context.title: Fix #3301 -- Disallowed 'javascript:' URLs in URLField validation.

description: Users could bypass XSS filters by providing 'javascript:alert(1)' in a field that was later rendered in an <a> tag's href.
general_discussion: While the template should escape, the validator should also catch this payload at the source.
Defense in depth.

context.title: Fix: Avoid race condition in file upload name collision handling.

description: When two files with the same name were uploaded simultaneously, the 'get_available_name' check could return the same name for both due to a race.
general_discussion: This resulted in one file overwriting the other.
Integrity bug, but not an exploit as it's a random collision.

comments: Could an attacker exploit this to overwrite critical files?
No, the storage is restricted to a specific media root and only appends suffixes; it cannot path-traverse.

context.title: Security: Blocked potential HTTP Response Splitting via CRLF in headers.

description: HttpResponse now raises an error if header values contain newline characters (\r or \n).
general_discussion: Attackers use CRLF injection to inject their own headers or a second response body.
Sanitization added to the base Response class.

context.title: Refactor: Use a thread-safe random number generator for temporary file names.

description: Switching from 'random.random()' to 'secrets.SystemRandom()' for generating temp file names.
general_discussion: Prevents filename prediction in multi-tenant environments.
Good for both security and avoiding collisions.

context.title: Fix: Corrected sign-extension bug in C-extension for GEOS bindings.

description: A signed integer was being promoted to unsigned incorrectly, leading to a buffer overflow on 32-bit systems.
general_discussion: This could allow an attacker to crash the Python interpreter with a crafted geometry object.
Memory safety issue. Closing and fixing in a private security branch.

comments: This is an A08/A06 risk. Needs CVE.

context.title: Chore: Updated internal CA bundle for development testing.

description: The mock certificates used in the test suite were expired. Updated them to 2030.
general_discussion: This only affects the 'tests/' directory. No impact on the framework core.
Standard maintenance.

context.title: Fix: Disable auto-fill for sensitive fields in Admin forms.

description: Added 'autocomplete="off"' to password and API key fields in the admin panel.
general_discussion: Prevents shared computers from remembering sensitive credentials in the browser cache.
Standard UX/Security hardening.

context.title: Fix: Optimized QuerySet.count() by removing unnecessary ORDER BY clauses.

description: Counting records doesn't require sorting. Removing the order_by() calls significantly reduces CPU load on the database for large tables.
general_discussion: This was causing timeouts on the dashboard for our biggest clients.
Great performance win.
Is there any security risk in changing the SQL structure?
No, the resulting set count is identical, just faster.

context.title: Security: Mitigated HTTP Parameter Pollution in MultiValueDict query parsing.

description: Certain middleware was only checking the first occurrence of a key, while the application used the last, allowing attackers to bypass security filters.
general_discussion: Standardizing on the last-key-wins approach to align with WAF expectations.
Prevents logic bypass via duplicate keys.

comments: Does this affect GET and POST?
Yes, both.

context.title: Fix: Prevented potential ReDoS in EmailValidator local-part regex.

description: A crafted email address with nested comments could trigger catastrophic backtracking in the validator.
general_discussion: An unauthenticated user could hang a signup process with a single request.
Regex refactored to be linear.

context.title: Refactor: Added type hints to the core authentication backends.

description: Adding PEP 484 type hints to ModelBackend to improve IDE support and catch static errors during development.
general_discussion: No functional changes, just static typing.
Helps developers avoid passing None to methods that expect a User instance.

context.title: Security: Added protection against session fixation during login.

description: Django now forces a session cycle (deleting the old session and creating a new one) whenever a user logs in.
general_discussion: Prevents an attacker from providing a pre-known session ID to a victim and taking over after they authenticate.
Hardening for the login() function.

context.title: Fix: Disallowed insecure redirects in the 'next' parameter of LoginView.

description: LoginView was allowing redirects to external URLs (e.g., //attacker.com). Now strictly validates against ALLOWED_HOSTS.
general_discussion: Prevents Open Redirect vulnerabilities used in phishing campaigns.
We use url_has_allowed_host_and_scheme() for validation now.

context.title: Refactor: Move internal constant-time comparison to a shared utility.

description: Consolidating constant_time_compare implementations from auth and sessions into django.utils.crypto.
general_discussion: DRY cleanup. No logic change.
Just making the utility more accessible for third-party apps.

context.title: Fix: Avoided race condition in cache-based session backend.

description: The cache.set() was overwriting sessions in a way that could drop data if two requests finished at the exact same millisecond.
general_discussion: This leads to users getting logged out intermittently under high load.
Functional integrity fix, not an exploit vector.

comments: Does this leak data between users?
No, it just loses the update for the current user (Fail-Safe).

context.title: Security: Sanitize SVG files in FileField to prevent stored XSS.

description: Added a check to strip <script> and event handlers from uploaded SVG files.
general_discussion: SVGs are XML and can contain executable code.
Crucial for sites allowing user-uploaded avatars or icons.

context.title: Fix: Ensure CSRF cookie is sent with 'SameSite=Lax' by default.

description: Updating the default cookie policy to protect against cross-site request forgery even in browsers that don't fully support our custom tokens.
general_discussion: Good hardening measure for modern browsers.
Reduces the attack surface significantly.

context.title: Refactor: Replaced MD5 with SHA-256 for internal cache keys.

description: While cache keys aren't a security boundary, MD5 is increasingly flagged by automated compliance scanners (FIPS).
general_discussion: Moving to SHA-256 to satisfy audit requirements.
No security exploit was found, just a compliance refactor.

context.title: Security: Prevented timing attack in password reset token validation.

description: The token comparison was short-circuiting, allowing an attacker to guess tokens character by character.
general_discussion: The leak was small (nanoseconds) but measurable over thousands of requests.
Implemented constant-time compare.

context.title: Fix #8801 -- Properly handle 'None' values in Admin change list filters.

description: A bug in the integer filter caused a crash (ValueError) when a 'None' string was passed in the URL.
general_discussion: Fixes a 500 error when clicking certain admin filters.
Functional bug in data casting.

comments: Can this lead to SQL injection?
No, it uses Django's ORM parameterization. It just crashes before reaching the DB.

context.title: Security: Restricted 'file://' scheme in URLField for certain backends.

description: Some server-side components (like image processors) were following 'file://' URLs, leading to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) and local file disclosure.
general_discussion: Attackers could point a URLField to /etc/passwd.
Added a blacklist for the 'file' scheme.

context.title: Chore: Updated the list of TLDs in EmailValidator.

description: Added support for new top-level domains like .app and .dev to the validation regex.
general_discussion: Just a maintenance update for the domain list.
Ensures legitimate users aren't blocked.

context.title: Security: Mitigated HTTP Header Injection via newline in Set-Cookie.

description: If a user-provided value (like a 'theme' name) was placed in a cookie without sanitization, it could inject additional headers.
general_discussion: Standard CRLF injection fix for the cookie engine.
Ensures response integrity.

context.title: Fix: Added defensive checks in JSON deserialization for large integers.

description: Very large integers in JSON could lead to CPU exhaustion during conversion to Python's arbitrary-precision ints (DoS).
general_discussion: Added a limit to the number of digits allowed in a JSON number.
Standard DoS protection for parsers.

context.title: Refactor: Optimized i18n URL prefixes for better SEO.

description: Changes how /en-us/ is handled to avoid double redirects. No change to access control logic.
general_discussion: Performance and SEO improvement.
Purely a routing refactor.

context.title: Security: Enforce permission check in Admin 'History' view for related objects.

description: The history view was showing logs for objects the user didn't have 'view' permission for, as long as they knew the ID.
general_discussion: Information leak in the admin logs.
Access control fix (A01).

context.title: Fix: Ensured temporary upload files are deleted on request termination.

description: If a request crashed mid-upload, the temp file remained on disk. Added a try/finally block to ensure cleanup.
general_discussion: Fixes a disk space leak issue.
Resource management bug (Fail-Safe).
If we don't fix this, can an attacker fill the disk?
Technically yes, but it would take millions of failed requests; still, it's a bug, not a vulnerability.
